rulings.Case Title: ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE, INC. VS. SILAUTUA S. FATILUA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of And Mtn For An Assignment Order And Accounting Of Funds
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 1, PLAINTIFF ATLANTIC CREDIT AND FINANCE, INC.'S Notice Of And Motion For An Assignment Order And Accounting Of Funds Granted in part. No opposition was filed. The Court grants the request for assignment. The Court denies the request for accounting of funds. The Court will sign the proposed order submitted with the moving papers, deleting paragraph 4 regarding accounting of funds, and modifying the order to reflect that the right to payment may be assigned only to the extent necessary to satisfy the money judgment, pursuant to Civil Procedure Code Section 708.510. rulings.Case Title: KAREN Y UCHIYAMA ESQ VS. EFRAIN GONZALEZ et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 9, DEFENDANT EFRAIN GONZALEZ' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT The motion for summary judgment is granted. The undisputed evidence demonstrates that the parties did not have a written contract and the two year statute of limitations of CCP 339 applies to this case. The two year statute of limitations also applies to the account stated claim under Iverson, Yoakum, Papiano & Hatch v. Berwald (1999) 76 Cal.App.4th 990, 995-997. Plaintiff's claims are time barred. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: ILEANA A. TORUNO VS. BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC., et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Amended Responses To Req For Production Of Docs Set Two
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 1: Defendant BLUEPRINT STUDIOS TRENDS INC.'S Motion to Compel Amended Responses to Request for Production of Documents Set Two Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel amended responses to defendant's second set request for production of documents is granted to the extent that the documents sought are under plaintiff's control and may be produced by her, said documents to be produced by plaintiff by no later than September 25, 2013. Defendant's request for sanctions is denied. Both parties are ordered to engage in a meet and confer again on these discovery matters by no later than September 20, 2013. Plaintiff claims that she is owed unpaid overtime wages, missed meal and rest break wages and statutory penalties because she was misclassified by defendant as an independent contractor. Defendant has unconditionally tendered to plaintiff $12,500. which represented all the wages she claims she is owed in this matter. Apparently, plaintiff has been unwilling to specify an amount which she now continues to seek from defendant which has hindered further settlement discussions. Under the circumstances the requested documents are relevant to defendant's defense and must be produced by plaintiff unless they are truly not under her control, a matter which must be resolved by the parties at the ordered meet and confer. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: VICTORIA PETERSON VS. G. MAZZERA COMPANY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories And For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 2: Defendant G. MAZZERA COMPANY'S Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories and for Monetary Sanctions Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: This is a motion by defendant to compel responses to special interrogatories and for monetary sanctions. Plaintiff, acting pro per, brought this personal injury/habitability action. The motion is unopposed and is hereby granted in all respects. Said responses are to be served by Plaintiff by no later than September 20, 2013 and the monetary sanctions paid by plaintiff by no later than September 25, 2013. It appears from defendant's motion papers that there is a history here of plaintiff failing to timely or otherwise respond to defendant's discovery requests and defendant's attempts to meet and confer with plaintiff on discovery matters starting in early May, 2013. On May 24, 2103, Judge Robertson signed an order continuing defendant's motion to compel plaintiff's attendance at a deposition to August 7, 2013. On May 16, 2013, Judge Robertson signed an order for monetary sanctions against plaintiff in the amount of $585. with respect to a submission by her of untimely interrogatory responses. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JANET HAAS VS. CHRISTOPHER I. MCCOLE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn For Protective Order And Modifying The Deposition Subpoenas For Production Of Business Records
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 3: Plaintiff JANET HAAS' Motion for Protective Order and Modifying the Deposition Subpoenas for Production of Business Records Pro Tem Judge Adrian Colley, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: In this action by plaintiff, pro per, for damages to plaintiff from injuries resulting from a collision of the vehicles of plaintiff and defendant, plaintiff has made a motion for "protective order and modifying the deposition subpoenas for production of business records". Plaintiff's motion is denied without prejudice as having been untimely served and filed on plaintiff, and, as a precondition to plaintiff's making another such motion, plaintiff and defendant are ordered to meet and confer no later than October 1, 2013, with respect to these discovery matters and to specify the time and other parameters of the discovery requested. Contrary to plaintiff's assertion, defendant's counsel's notice of unavailability was permissible. While every effort is to be made to accommodate pro per plaintiffs, they too must follow statutory procedural requirements which plaintiff did not do here. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to adrianacolley@aol.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: JEFFREY JONG VS. 1ST STRING ATHLETICS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 13, DEFENDANTS DMITRY DEREVYANKO, RAYANNAH SALAHUDDIN'S DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT Overruled. Plaintiff has pled sufficient facts to allege each cause of action. The allegations of alter ego are sufficiently pled. It cannot be determined on the face of the Complaint that Plaintiff's claims are unripe or barred by the statute of limitations. Defendants raise factual issues pertaining to Plaintiff's employment that are not the proper subject of a demurrer. The pleadings are not uncertain. rulings.Case Title: CONNOR WHITMAN VS. PLAYLAND ABC, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Amend Complaint To Add Brian Koski As Doe 11
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 21, PLAINTIFF CONNOR WHITMAN'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Amend Complaint To Add Brian Koski As Doe 11 Granted. No opposition was filed. The Court will sign the order submitted with the moving papers. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/MJM)

rulings.Case Title: THE SOMA GRAND RESIDENCES OWNERS ASSOCIATION, et al VS. 1160 MISSION ASSOCIATES, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on Calendar for Monday, September 9, 2013, Line 22, DEFENDANTS 1160 MISSION ASSOCIATES, LLC, AGI-TMG MISSION, LLC, TMG 1160, LLC, WEBCOR BUILDERS, INC.'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT The demurrer is off calendar as moot. The demurrer was directed to the second cause of action and plaintiff dismissed that claim. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. =(302/MJM)
